title: rosa canina var. cantabrica
intro: Rosa canina var. cantabrica, also known as the Cantabrian rose or Cantabric rose, is a specific variety of the Rosa canina species, a shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions due to its high content of vitamin C and antioxidants. Rosa canina var. cantabrica is rich in gallic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as improved circulation, reduced inflammation, and boosted immune system function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, and its oil can be extracted for topical use in skincare products and wound healing remedies.
